excerpt.rendered: Sebastian Neumaier firstname.lastname@fhstp.ac.at(replace first- and lastname) Fachhochschule St. Pölten Forschungsgruppe Data Intelligence Department Informatik und Security Campus-Platz 1, 3100 St. Pölten

content.rendered:
I am a computer science researcher, living in Vienna, Austria, working at the St. Pölten University of Applied Sciences.

In my current research I focus on different aspects of data management:

  • Data ecosystems: data architectures, data governance, data quality management.
  • Knowledge Graphs: information extraction and modelling with semantic technologies.

I defended my Ph.D. in Computer Science in 2019 at the Vienna University of Technology; my thesis is centered around methods to integrate and enrich open data using semantic web– and machine learning-based technologies, supervised by Professor Axel Polleres.

News

  • We successfully wrapped up the second edition of up the Knowledge Graphs and Neurosymbolic AI Workshop, this time co-located with SEMANTiCS 2025, in Vienna, September 03 – 05, 2025. Here’s the organizing team!
  • The Horizon Europe funded project CERTAIN – Certification for Ethical and Regulatory Transparency in Artificial Intelligence – had its kickoff on January 24, 2025!
  • We have two netidee projects starting in 2025: CrOSSD2 and HaSPI
  • I co-organized the Knowledge Graphs and Neurosymbolic AI workshop at AIRoV, the First Austrian Symposium on AI, Robotics, and Vision, on 25.-27.3.2024, located in Innsbruck, Austria.
  • I co-organized the Workshop on Usage Control Policies in Dataspaces on May 2, 2023, located at the 5th International Data Science Conference (iDSC).
  • New project called “Towards a Critical Open-Source Software Database” funded by netidee (Internetstiftung) started in April 2023.
  • The proceedings of the 2022 SEMANTiCS conference “Towards a Knowledge-Aware AI” are available as open access.
  • I co-organized the First Workshop on Semantic AI (SemAI 2022) on September 12, 2022, located at SEMANTiCS conference 2022.
  • The FFG funded project DiDaMe – Towards a Distributed Data Mesh Platform – had its kickoff on March 1, 2022! Project poster: [pdf]
